title: Gemälde und ihre Meister
summaries: "Gemälde und ihre Meister" by Arnold Reimann is a collection of art critiques and educational essays written in the early 20th century. The book focuses on the interpretation and appreciation of renowned paintings and their creators, aiming to impart an understanding of art to the youth and general public. It serves as a guide for recognizing and valuing artistic expression, emphasizing the relationship between art and the cultural identity of the time.  The opening of the work features a preface by Reimann, where he reflects on the societal changes brought about by industrialization and the need for a cultural renaissance through art. He expresses concerns about the loss of spiritual depth in contemporary life and advocates for a return to an appreciation of beauty and the emotional power of art. Reimann outlines the book's intention to awaken a sense of reverence in children for masterpieces and their creators, encouraging readers to engage with art in a way that breathes life into the works, transforming them into personal experiences of beauty and meaning.
